# Aggregate Survey Biomass {#aggregate_biomass}

```{r echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = F)
library(ecodata)
library(downloadthis)
```

*Last updated October 22, 2025*

**Description**: Aggregate biomass from Northeast Fisheries Science Center (NEFSC) bottom trawl survey.

**Contributor(s)**: Sean Lucey, Andrew Beet, and Sarah Gaichas.

**Affiliations**: NEFSC

**Indicator Family**: 

- [X] Megafauna


**Indicator Category**:

- [X] Database pull
- [X] Database pull with analysis


**Synthesis Theme**:

- [X] Multiple System Drivers


```{r download_aggregate_biomass}
# Download dataset from ecodata
try(downloadthis::download_this(
ecodata::aggregate_biomass,
output_name = 'aggregate_biomass',
output_extension = '.csv',
button_label = 'Download dataset from `ecodata`',
button_type = 'default',
has_icon = TRUE,
icon = 'fa fa-save',
class = 'hvr-sweep-to-left',
csv2 = F),
silent = TRUE)
```

## Introduction to Indicator
The NEFSC has been conducting bi-annual bottom trawl surveys along the Northeast US Continental Shelf for over 60 years.  The survey is conducted in the spring and fall of each year.  The fall survey began in 1964 and the spring in 1967.  The survey is designed as a stratified random survey.  We have calculated stratified means with sets of survey strata that closely align with our Ecosystem Production Unit boundaries.  Species are aggregated into feeding guilds in order to gauge the relative stability and health of the system.

## Key Results and Visualizations
Aggregate biomass levels have been relatively stable over time. Black points represent data from the Northeast Fisheries Science Center (NEFSC) bottom trawl survey. Red points represent data from the NEAMAP inshore survey.

**Mid-Atlantic Bight**
```{r plot_aggregate_biomassMABMidAtlantic}
# Plot indicator
ggplotObject <- ecodata::plot_aggregate_biomass(EPU = 'MAB', report = 'MidAtlantic', n = 10)
ggplotObject
try(downloadthis::download_this(
ggplotObject$data,
output_name = 'aggregate_biomassMABMidAtlantic',
output_extension = '.csv',
button_label = 'Download the data plotted above',
button_type = 'default',
has_icon = TRUE,
icon = 'fa fa-save',
class = 'hvr-sweep-to-left',
csv2 = F),
silent = TRUE)
```
<br>

**Georges Bank**
```{r plot_aggregate_biomassGBNewEngland}
# Plot indicator
ggplotObject <- ecodata::plot_aggregate_biomass(EPU = 'GB', report = 'NewEngland', n = 10)
ggplotObject
try(downloadthis::download_this(
ggplotObject$data,
output_name = 'aggregate_biomassGBNewEngland',
output_extension = '.csv',
button_label = 'Download the data plotted above',
button_type = 'default',
has_icon = TRUE,
icon = 'fa fa-save',
class = 'hvr-sweep-to-left',
csv2 = F),
silent = TRUE)
```
<br>

**Gulf of Maine**
```{r plot_aggregate_biomassGOMNewEngland}
# Plot indicator
ggplotObject <- ecodata::plot_aggregate_biomass(EPU = 'GOM', report = 'NewEngland', n = 10)
ggplotObject
try(downloadthis::download_this(
ggplotObject$data,
output_name = 'aggregate_biomassGOMNewEngland',
output_extension = '.csv',
button_label = 'Download the data plotted above',
button_type = 'default',
has_icon = TRUE,
icon = 'fa fa-save',
class = 'hvr-sweep-to-left',
csv2 = F),
silent = TRUE)
```
<br>

## Implications
Aggregate biomass is a holistic indicator that reveals the underlying ecosystem that the fisheries operates within.  While there has been evidence of overfishing of key commercial species, the overall ecosystem is relatively stable.

## Indicator statistics 
Spatial scale: By EPU

Temporal scale: Spring (March-May) and Fall (September-November)

**Variable definitions**

```{r vars_aggregate_biomass}
# Pull all var names
vars <- ecodata::aggregate_biomass |>
   dplyr::select(Var, Units) |>
   dplyr::distinct()

DT::datatable(vars)
```
## Get the data

**Point of contact**: [Andrew Beet (Andrew.Beet@NOAA.gov)](mailto:Andrew Beet (Andrew.Beet@NOAA.gov)){.email}

**ecodata dataset**: `ecodata::aggregate_biomass`

**Tech Doc link**: <https://noaa-edab.github.io/tech-doc/aggregate_biomass.html>

### Public Availability

Source data are publicly available.
